commonProblems.h2: Audi A6 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: 2.0 TDI EGR Cooler Failure - Why CAHA/CNHA Diesel Engines Clog Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Audi A6 C7 2.0 TDI (2011-2018, CAHA & CNHA), Audi A6 C8 2.0 TDI (2018-present, CNHA/CZJA)
problems.rootCause: The EGR cooler becomes blocked with carbon deposits from exhaust recirculation, reducing coolant flow and causing overheating. On Euro 6 engines (CNHA/CZJA), the additional AdBlue injection system accelerates crystallisation inside the cooler.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chemical cleaning of cooler and EGR valve, adaption reset
New OEM or upgraded stainless steel cooler, new gaskets
Complete rebuilt unit with upgraded EGR system, new cooler, supply & fit

problems.recommendation: On a C7 A6 worth £4,000-£8,000, a specialist EGR cooler replacement with an upgraded stainless steel unit is the smartest investment - it resolves the root cause permanently. Dealer cleaning is a short-term patch that rarely lasts beyond 12 months. If the cooler has already failed catastrophically and contaminated the oil or caused head gasket damage, a full rebuilt engine is the only reliable path.
problems.cta: Compare CAHA & CNHA rebuilt engine prices for your Audi A6

problems.h4: 2.0 TDI Oil Pump Drive Failure - Why BLB/BRE Engines Self-Destruct
problems.affectedModels: Audi A6 C6 2.0 TDI (2004-2008, BLB & BRE engine codes)
problems.rootCause: The oil pump is driven by a hexagonal key that rounds off over time, especially on long-life service intervals. Once the drive fails, oil pressure drops to zero and the turbo, camshaft, and bottom end are destroyed within seconds.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace hex key with upgraded steel drive, new oil pump if worn, sump clean
New oil pump, replacement turbo, oil system flush, crankshaft inspection
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles - risk of hidden bearing damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt unit with upgraded oil pump drive, new turbo, supply & fit

problems.recommendation: On a BLB/BRE-powered C6 A6 worth £2,000-£4,000, preventative oil pump drive upgrade is essential if no failure symptoms exist. Once the hex key has rounded, emergency repair is risky - debris often damages bearings silently. We recommend a specialist rebuilt engine with upgraded drive components, which costs only slightly more than emergency repair but delivers a fresh long-life unit.
problems.cta: Get quotes for BLB/BRE rebuilt engines with upgraded oil pump drive

problems.h4: 3.0 TDI Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why BMK/CDYA V6 Engines Rattle
problems.affectedModels: Audi A6 C6 3.0 TDI Quattro (2004-2009, BMK), Audi A6 C6 3.0 TDI (2008-2011, CDYA/CDYB)
problems.rootCause: The timing chain tensioners on the rear-mounted chain assembly lose oil pressure and fail to maintain tension. The chain slaps against guides until they shatter, causing valve-to-piston contact and catastrophic engine destruction. Early BMK units are most vulnerable.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all chains, tensioners, guides (rear of engine, gearbox removal required)
Complete rebuilt unit with new timing chains, upgraded tensioners, supply & fit

problems.recommendation: On a C6 3.0 TDI Quattro worth £3,000-£6,000, preventative timing chain replacement is worthwhile if caught early - but only at a specialist with specific C6 3.0 TDI experience. Dealer pricing often exceeds the car's value once labour is added. If the engine has already jumped timing or shows metal in the oil, do not attempt repair - go straight to a rebuilt unit from a specialist.
problems.cta: Compare 3.0 TDI rebuilt engine prices for your Audi A6 Quattro

problems.h4: 2.0 TFSI Oil Consumption - Why CDNB/CAEB Petrol Engines Burn Oil
problems.affectedModels: Audi A6 C6 2.0 TFSI (2008-2011, CAEB), Audi A6 C7 2.0 TFSI (2011-2015, CDNB)
problems.rootCause: The low-tension piston rings become clogged with carbon deposits, losing their ability to seal against the cylinder bore. Oil then bypasses the rings and burns in the combustion chamber, with consumption exceeding 1 litre per 1,000 miles in severe cases.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chemical decarbonisation of piston rings via spark plug holes, oil flush
Engine strip, new piston rings (updated design), cylinder hone, new bolts
Complete rebuilt unit with upgraded piston rings, new turbo, supply & fit

problems.recommendation: On a C7 2.0 TFSI worth £5,000-£10,000, a specialist piston ring replacement using updated rings is the correct permanent fix - dealer pricing is often double the car's residual value. Avoid chemical decoke treatments; they rarely work on this failure. If the engine has suffered oil starvation damage to the turbo or timing chain, move straight to a rebuilt unit.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CDNB/CAEB rebuilt engines with upgraded piston rings

problems.h4: 3.0 TDI Injector Failure - Why C6 & C7 V6 Diesels Misfire and Smoke
problems.affectedModels: Audi A6 C6 3.0 TDI (2004-2011, BMK/CDYA), Audi A6 C7 3.0 TDI (2011-2018, CDUD/CRTE)
problems.rootCause: Piezo injectors on the common-rail system develop internal leakage or solenoid wear, causing over-fuelling, white smoke on startup, rough idle, and eventual cylinder wash-down which dilutes the oil and destroys bearings.

repairOptions.longevity: 50,000-80,000 miles - other injectors likely to follow

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all 6 injectors (remanufactured or new), rail seals, coding
Complete rebuilt unit with new injectors, fuel system flush, supply & fit

problems.recommendation: On a C7 3.0 TDI worth £5,000-£12,000, replace all six injectors via a specialist using high-quality remanufactured units - mixing old and new injectors often leads to continued imbalance. If the engine has suffered cylinder wash-down and you notice diesel smell in the oil, do not run it. A rebuilt engine is the only safe option once bearing damage is suspected.
problems.cta: Compare CDUD & CRTE rebuilt engine prices for your 3.0 TDI A6

problems.h4: AdBlue System Failure - Why C8 2.0 TDI Diesels Trigger Warning Lights
problems.affectedModels: Audi A6 C8 2.0 TDI (2018-present, CNHA/CZJA), Audi A6 C8 40 TDI (2019-present, DFBA)
problems.rootCause: The AdBlue injector crystallises when the engine is switched off mid-regeneration, blocking the injector nozzle. The SCR catalyst then fails to treat NOx emissions, triggering a "no restart in X miles" countdown on the dashboard.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Ultrasonic cleaning of injector, system reset, forced regeneration
New AdBlue injector, pump, tank heater if failed, software adaptation
Complete rebuilt unit with new AdBlue system components, supply & fit

problems.recommendation: On a C8 A6 still under manufacturer warranty (typically up to 3 years), push the dealer for goodwill contribution on AdBlue system failure - it's a known weakness. For out-of-warranty cars, specialist injector replacement is the value option. Do not ignore the countdown; once the system locks the engine, recovery and forced dealer reset adds significant cost. Replacement of the complete engine is rarely needed for isolated AdBlue faults.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CNHA/CZJA rebuilt engines with AdBlue system warranty coverage

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Audi A6
finalCta.paragraph: A failed engine doesn't have to mean the end of your A6. Whether you're facing EGR cooler blockage on a 2.0 TDI, oil pump failure on a C6, or timing chain tensioner failure on a 3.0 TDI V6, EnginesMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Audi A6 specialists. Every rebuilt engine comes with upgraded components targeting the original failure point, a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today - get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Audi A6 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EnginesMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Audi A6 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

backBullets: Every A6 2.0 TDI (CAHA/CNHA) gets upgraded oil pump drive

engineSizes.h2: Audi A6 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant

items.title: Audi A6 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9 TDI powered early C4 and C5 generation A6 models throughout the 1990s, establishing Audi's reputation for durable, economical diesel motoring. The 110bhp AFN/AVG units became the benchmark for executive diesel efficiency, while earlier 1Z/AHU offered simpler, bombproof mechanical injection. These engines are now rare but still sought after by high-mileage users.
commonFailurePoints: injector pump seal wear at high mileage
items.cta: Get quotes for Audi A6 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Audi A6 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0 TDI became the volume diesel for the A6 from the C6 generation onward, powering countless fleet and private hire vehicles across the UK. Early BLB/BRE units (2004-2008) gained a poor reputation for oil pump drive failure, while later CAHA/CNHA (2011-2018) brought Euro 6 compliance but introduced EGR cooler clogging and AdBlue system faults on C8 models.
items.cta: Get quotes for Audi A6 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Audi A6 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.5 TDI V6 (AFB/AKM) and the rarer 5-cylinder AEL offered a smoother alternative to the 1.9 four-cylinder in C5-generation A6 models. The V6 brought six-cylinder refinement to the diesel range but introduced more complex injection systems and turbocharger components that demand specialist attention today.
items.cta: Get quotes for Audi A6 2.5L diesel engines

items.title: Audi A6 2.7L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.7 TDI V6 was a C6-generation exclusive, effectively a de-stroked version of the 3.0 TDI offering improved fuel economy with most of the V6 refinement. The CANA/CANB units produced 180-190hp and were popular with buyers wanting V6 smoothness without the running costs of the 3.0 litre.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain rattle on early production
items.cta: Get quotes for Audi A6 2.7L diesel engines

items.title: Audi A6 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0 TDI V6 is the definitive A6 diesel engine - torquey, smooth and the heart of Quattro models across C6, C7 and C8 generations. Early BMK units (2004-2009) suffer timing chain tensioner failure at the rear of the engine, while later CDUD/CRTE and CLZB units brought emissions compliance and mild hybrid assistance on C8 models.
items.cta: Get quotes for Audi A6 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Audi A6 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8T turbocharged four-cylinder appeared in late C5 and early C6 A6 models, offering surprising performance from a small displacement. The 1.8T was the entry-level petrol option for buyers who wanted forced induction without stepping up to V6 running costs, though direct injection engines (CDNB) later brought oil consumption problems.
items.cta: Compare Audi A6 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi A6 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0 TFSI became the main petrol option for C6 and C7 A6 models, offering 180-211hp from a turbocharged direct-injection four-cylinder. While responsive and economical for its power output, these engines developed a well-earned reputation for excessive oil consumption caused by carbon-clogged piston rings - a failure that often leads to full engine replacement.
commonFailurePoints: Piston ring carbon clogging causing oil consumption
items.cta: Compare Audi A6 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi A6 2.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.4 V6 was the naturally aspirated entry-level six-cylinder for C5 and early C6 A6 models, offering smoother power delivery than the 1.8T without the complexity of forced induction. The 2.4 appealed to buyers prioritising refinement over outright performance, though fuel economy lagged behind the turbocharged four-cylinder options.
commonFailurePoints: oil leaks from camshaft adjuster seals
items.cta: Compare Audi A6 2.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi A6 2.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.8 V6 sat between the 2.4 and 3.0 in the C5 and early C6 petrol lineups, offering a power bump without the fuel penalty of the larger 3.0. These naturally aspirated units were among the smoothest in the range but are now relatively rare in the UK replacement market.
items.cta: Compare Audi A6 2.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi A6 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The supercharged 3.0 TFSI V6 (CREC/CGWB) delivered 310-333hp to C7 Quattro models with linear, lag-free power that owners still rate highly. These engines are more reliable than the 2.0 TFSI but suffer from PCV failure, water pump leaks, and supercharger clutch wear as they approach higher mileages. C8 models moved to turbocharged 3.0 V6 units with mild hybrid assistance.
items.cta: Compare Audi A6 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi A6 4.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.2 V8 powered the C5 and C6-generation S6 models, delivering 290-335hp of naturally aspirated V8 character. The 4.2 remains a cult favourite among enthusiasts, but timing chain service at the rear of the engine requires gearbox removal - a labour cost that often exceeds the car's value. These engines are for committed owners only.
commonFailurePoints: oil consumption from valve guide wear
items.cta: Compare Audi A6 4.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Audi A6 3.0L MHEV Engines
items.description: The C8 generation introduced 48V mild hybrid technology to the 3.0 TDI (CLZB) and 3.0 TFSI ranges, using a belt-driven starter generator to enable coasting and smoother stop-start operation. While the combustion engine remains conventional, the 48V system adds calibration complexity and a second battery that requires specialist handling during replacement. Not sure which engine size your Audi A6 needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EnginesMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Audi A6 specialists.
commonFailurePoints: system calibration loss after engine replacement

items.title: Audi A6 Diesel Engines
items.description: The backbone of UK A6 sales across four generations, diesel engines dominate replacement enquiries - from 1.9 TDI simplicity to complex 3.0 V6 units with AdBlue and 48V systems.
items.descriptor: The backbone of UK A6 sales across four generations, diesel engines dominate replacement enquiries - from 1.9 TDI simplicity to complex 3.0 V6 units with AdBlue and 48V systems.
items.familiesLabel: Common Audi A6 Diesel Engine Families
families: 1Z/AHU/AFN/AVG - 1.9 TDI, bombproof mechanical injection for C4/C5
BLB/BRE - 2.0 TDI, oil pump drive hex key failure on C6 (2004-2008)
CANA/CANB - 2.7 TDI V6, C6-only unit with injector weakness
CAHA/CNHA - 2.0 TDI Euro 5/6, fleet favourite with EGR cooler clogging
BMK/CDYA/CDYB - 3.0 TDI V6, timing chain tensioner failure on early units
CDUD/CRTE - 3.0 TDI, turbo actuator wear on C7 Quattro models
CLZB - 3.0 TDI MHEV, 48V system integration for C8
knownFor: Excellent motorway fuel economy (50-60mpg real world)

EGR cooler clogging and AdBlue faults on Euro 6 units
3.0 TDI timing chain tensioner failure at 90k-140k miles

engineYears.h2: Audi A6 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: From the 1994 C4 to the 2025 C9 generation, this timeline covers every engine change across UK-market Audi A6 models - helping you identify your engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The first-generation A6 replaced the Audi 100, launching with carryover engines before introducing the legendary 2.2 turbo and 4.2 V8 in S6 form.
keyChanges: S6 introduced with 2.2 turbo 5-cylinder and 4.2 V8
Saloon and Avant (estate) body styles available
Quattro all-wheel drive optional across range
knownFor: Built on legendary Audi 100 platform - robust and durable
1.9 TDI (1Z/AHU) is bombproof, still sought after today
2.2 turbo 5-cylinder (AAN) is a cult classic engine
4.2 V8 timing chain service requires engine-out work
years.cta: Get quotes for 1994-1997 Audi A6 replacement engines
years.ticker: Audi A6 1.9 TDI engine replacement • Audi S6 2.2 turbo rebuild • Audi A6 C4 engine compatibility

years.description: The C5 generation brought more powerful V6 diesel options, the first 2.5 TDI V6, and a broader petrol lineup including the 1.8T and 4.2 V8 S6.
keyChanges: Multitronic CVT introduced on front-wheel drive models
Facelift (2001) brought revised styling and engines
knownFor: 2.5 TDI V6 smoother than 5-cylinder but injector-prone
1.8T offers tunable performance on a budget
Multitronic CVT has reliability concerns at high mileage
C5 facelift models (2001-2004) hold up better
years.cta: Compare C5 Audi A6 engine replacement prices
years.ticker: Audi A6 2.5 TDI injector failure • Audi A6 1.8T engine rebuild • C5 A6 Multitronic compatibility

years.description: The C6 generation debuted with 2.0 TDI (BLB/BRE) diesel and 2.0 TFSI petrol - but the BLB/BRE oil pump drive failure would become a major replacement driver.
keyChanges: All-new MLB platform with improved aerodynamics
2.0 TDI (BLB/BRE) replaces 1.9 TDI as volume diesel
S6 gains 5.2 V10 from Lamborghini Gallardo
knownFor: BLB/BRE oil pump drive hex key failure is catastrophic
2.7 TDI is a C6-exclusive engine with injector weaknesses
3.0 TDI (BMK) suffers timing chain tensioner failure
5.2 V10 S6 is a future classic with very high running costs
years.cta: Get quotes for 2004-2008 Audi A6 replacement engines
years.ticker: Audi A6 2.0 TDI oil pump failure • BLB engine replacement cost • Audi A6 C6 2.7 TDI injector repair

Audi A6 C6 2.7 TDI injector repair

years.description: The C6 facelift brought updated diesel and petrol engines, including the more reliable 3.0 TDI (CDYA/CDYB) and the oil-hungry 2.0 TFSI (CAEB).
keyChanges: Facelift styling with LED daytime running lights
2.0 TDI BRE replaced by more refined units
mainEngines: CDYA/CDYB - 3.0L V6 diesel (240hp, improved timing chain)
knownFor: CAHA diesel improved reliability over BLB/BRE
3.0 TDI (CDYA/CDYB) still has timing chain concerns
2.0 TFSI (CAEB) oil consumption is a major issue
3.0 TFSI supercharged is the performance sweet spot
years.ticker: Audi A6 2.0 TFSI oil consumption fix • CAEB engine rebuild • Audi A6 3.0 TDI timing chain replacement

Audi A6 3.0 TDI timing chain replacement

years.description: The C7 generation introduced the MLB Evo platform, new 2.0 TDI (CNHA), and the supercharged 3.0 TFSI (CREC) that became a petrol favourite.
keyChanges: 2.0 TDI (CNHA) with 177hp - most searched UK code
knownFor: CNHA is the most searched A6 engine code in the UK
2.0 TFSI (CDNB) oil consumption continues from CAEB
3.0 TDI (CDUD) turbo actuator failure at 80k-120k miles
Supercharged 3.0 TFSI is smooth and reliable
Hybrid version had very limited UK sales

keyChanges: Facelift styling with matrix LED headlights
Euro 6 emissions compliance across diesel range
AdBlue injection introduced on 2.0 TDI (CNHA) and 3.0 TDI
Virtual cockpit introduced on later models
popularModels: Audi A6 2.0 TDI ultra S line
Audi A6 3.0 TDI Quattro Black Edition

2.0 TFSI (CYRB) improved oil control vs CDNB

DPF regeneration issues on short journeys
Black Edition trim most desirable for resale

years.description: The C8 generation introduced 48V mild hybrid across all engines, new 2.0 TDI (DFBA) with 204hp, and moved away from supercharging to turbo V6s.
keyChanges: MLB Evo platform with 48V mild hybrid on all engines
New 2.0 TDI (DFBA) with 204hp - most powerful 4-cylinder diesel
knownFor: 48V mild hybrid adds complexity and calibration needs
DFBA is the highest-output 4-cylinder diesel in A6 history
AdBlue injector crystallisation is a common fault

48V mild hybrid standard across all engines

faq.h2: Audi A6 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does an Audi A6 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Audi A6 engine typically costs £2,500-£5,000 supply-only, rising to £3,500-£7,000 with professional fitting. Prices vary by engine code, variant and rebuild quality.
keyPoints: 2.0 TDI (CAHA/CNHA) rebuilt units: £2,200-£3,500 supply-only
3.0 TDI V6 (CDUD/CRTE) rebuilt: £2,800-£4,500 supply-only
2.0 TFSI (CDNB/CYRB) rebuilt: £2,500-£4,000 supply-only
Supply & fit adds £450-£800 to engine price
Remanufactured units cost £5,000-£7,500, new engines £8,000+
items.cta: Compare Audi A6 engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is the 2.0 TFSI oil consumption problem really that bad on the Audi A6?
items.answer: Yes - the 2.0 TFSI (CAEB/CDNB, 2008-2015) is notorious for oil consumption reaching 1 litre per 1,000 miles due to faulty piston rings. Audi introduced a fix from 2015 onwards.
keyPoints: Affects 2008-2015 A6 2.0 TFSI models with CAEB and CDNB codes
Carbon-clogged piston rings cause oil to burn in combustion chamber
Blue exhaust smoke and low oil warnings are early symptoms
Permanent fix requires piston ring replacement (£2,200-£3,200 specialist)
items.cta: Get quotes for 2.0 TFSI rebuilt engines with upgraded piston rings

items.question: Should I repair or replace my Audi A6 3.0 TDI with timing chain failure?
items.answer: If the chain has snapped or jumped timing, replacement is usually the better choice. Repair costs £3,000+ for labour alone, and hidden valve damage is common once the chain fails.
keyPoints: Preventive timing chain replacement (before failure): £1,400-£2,200 specialist
Post-failure repair (chain snapped): £3,000-£5,000 with engine-out labour
Rebuilt 3.0 TDI engine: £2,800-£4,500 supply-only
Risk of bent valves increases repair cost significantly
On A6 worth £4,000-£7,000, replacement often beats repair
items.cta: Compare 3.0 TDI rebuilt engine prices for your Audi A6 Quattro

items.question: How long does a rebuilt Audi A6 engine last?
items.answer: A quality rebuilt Audi A6 engine lasts 75,000-150,000 miles - 8-12 years for most UK drivers. Lifespan depends entirely on rebuild quality and your maintenance habits.
keyPoints: Professional rebuild with OEM parts: 100,000+ miles typical
Proper break-in period (500-1,000 miles) critical for longevity
Regular oil changes every 5,000-7,500 miles extend life significantly
12-month unlimited mileage warranty included with every EngineMarket supply
Poor rebuilds may fail within 20,000-30,000 miles
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Audi A6 engine with 12-month warranty

items.question: Which Audi A6 engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The 3.0 TFSI supercharged V6 (CREC, 2011-2018) is widely considered the most reliable A6 engine, with robust construction and fewer emissions-related failures than diesel variants.
keyPoints: 3.0 TFSI supercharged: Very good reliability, PCV failure main issue (£500-£800)
2.0 TDI (CAHA/CNHA): Good but EGR cooler fails 60k-100k miles
3.0 TDI V6: Fair reliability - timing chain tensioner weak point
2.0 TFSI (pre-2015): Avoid - oil consumption problems widespread
Post-2015 2.0 TFSI: Improved piston rings fix the oil issue
items.cta: Get quotes for reliable Audi A6 replacement engines

items.question: What causes the AdBlue system fault and countdown to no start on my Audi A6?
items.answer: The AdBlue injector crystallises when the engine stops mid-regeneration, blocking the nozzle. The system then locks the engine from restarting once the countdown reaches zero.
keyPoints: Affects C8 2.0 TDI (CNHA/CZJA) and 3.0 TDI Euro 6 models
items.cta: Compare Audi A6 AdBlue system repair costs

items.question: What is the difference between rebuilt and reconditioned Audi A6 engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt means the engine is dismantled and worn parts replaced. Reconditioned (recon) means factory-grade restoration to OEM tolerances - typically higher quality and more expensive.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine: Worn bearings, rings, gaskets replaced - £1,600-£3,200
Reconditioned engine: Full strip, inspection, CNC machining - £1,800-£3,500
Remanufactured: Restored to factory specs with new components - £2,200-£4,200
Used engine: Removed from donor with basic testing - £900-£1,800
All EngineMarket suppliers provide minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs reconditioned Audi A6 engine prices

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a high-mileage Audi A6?
items.answer: Yes, if the rest of the car is sound. A £3,500-£5,000 rebuilt engine delivering 100,000 miles works out at 3.5p-5p per mile - far cheaper than replacing the car.
keyPoints: Rebuild worthwhile if A6 body, gearbox and electrics are in good condition
Typical break-even point: car worth £4,000-£7,000 after repair
New engine replacement (£8,000+) rarely makes economic sense
Used engine (£900-£1,800) is budget option but higher risk
Get a full vehicle inspection before committing to engine replacement
items.cta: Get a quote for Audi A6 engine replacement - no obligation

items.question: What are the most common Audi A6 2.0 TDI engine problems?
items.answer: EGR cooler clogging (CAHA/CNHA, 60k-100k miles) and oil pump drive failure (BLB/BRE, 2004-2008, 80k-120k miles) are the two most common - both can be catastrophic if ignored.
keyPoints: EGR cooler failure: Coolant loss, overheating, potential head gasket damage
Oil pump drive hex key failure (BLB/BRE): Zero oil pressure, destroys turbo and bottom end
DPF clogging on short journeys: Regeneration required every 300-500 miles
Injector failure on 2.7 TDI and 3.0 TDI: White smoke, rough idle
Upgrade to stainless steel EGR cooler prevents recurrence
items.cta: Compare 2.0 TDI rebuilt engine prices for your Audi A6

items.question: Can I get an Audi A6 engine replacement with fitting included?
items.answer: Yes - EngineMarket connects you with a nationwide network of Audi specialists offering supply & fit from £450-£800 added to the engine price, including mobile fitting for C6/C7 models.
keyPoints: Garage fitting available nationwide for all A6 generations
Mobile fitting available for C6 and C7 models (C8 requires garage)
Fitting includes old unit removal, new engine, coolant, oil and ECU coding
12-month labour warranty included on all specialist fitting
C8 mild hybrid units require specialist calibration of 48V system
items.cta: Find Audi A6 engine supply & fit deals near you
